title: The life and thought of Alexandre Kojève [Book Review]
author: Engelking

opening_move.description: The review opens by characterising the 'recognizable type' of twentieth-century European political philosopher who attracts fascination through proximity to power, and frames Kojève as the most compelling exemplar — positioning Filoni's biography as successfully navigating between reducing thought to biography and insulating it from life.
opening_move.stakes_claimed: A biographical approach to Kojève that holds together life and thought reveals the philosophical coherence behind his apparent paradoxes (theorist and technocrat; universal and particular)

rivals_engaged: biographical reductionism (thought subordinated to political proximity)
theoretical_positioning.description: Expository and appreciative review of Filoni's intellectual biography of Kojève. The reviewer presents Filoni's methodological via media and traces the biographical-philosophical narrative chronologically.

epistemic_mode.interpretive_methods: intellectual biography; close reading of Filoni's reconstruction of Kojève's life and thought
epistemic_mode.notes: Book review (4 pages). Primarily expository biographical narrative. No normative conclusion; the argument is about how to read Kojève's intellectual life, not about political theory claims.

case_type.case_name: Filoni, The Life and Thought of Alexandre Kojève (trans. Broder, 2025)

normative_stake.domain: intellectual history / methodology of political philosophy

confidence_notes: Book review (4 pages). Classified as purely_interpretive_hermeneutic: the review is entirely about how to read Kojève's intellectual biography with no normative political theory conclusion drawn.
